channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Musk, Ramaswamy ‘DOGE’ confidence in Supreme Court may be tested
item.description: Elon Musk and Vivek Ramaswamy are leaning on the conservative Supreme Court to support their ambitious plans to slash federal regulations and increase government efficiency.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, The Hill

item.title: AWS and Anthropic Strengthen Partnership, Paving Way for AI Advancements
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) and Anthropic expand their partnership, with AWS investing an additional $4 billion in the startup and using Trainium processors to train models.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The lingering questions around Nvidia’s strong quarterly report
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Technology Letter and is republished here with permission. Late Wednesday evening, some men were sitting around the Russian baths in Wall Street’s financial district talking about Nvidia.
item.dc:creator: Tiernan Ray, Fast Company

item.title: UK regulator says Apple, Google blocking innovation in mobile browsers
item.description: The United Kingdom’s top antitrust regulator said Friday that an independent inquiry provisionally found that Apple's and Google’s policies for mobile browsers are blocking innovation.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Gallagher not interested in serving as SEC chair in second Trump administration
item.description: Dan Gallagher, chief legal officer at the financial services firm Robinhood, said Friday that he is not interested in chairing the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) in President-elect Trump’s second administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Risk and Compliance Exchange 2024: DoD’ Stacy Bostjanick, DCMA’s Jennifer Henderson on finding ‘any means possible’ to help small biz with CMMC
item.description: As the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification program inches closer to reality, DoD prepares defense industrial base for compliance requirements.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: TypeScript 5.7 arrives with improved error reporting
item.description: TypeScript 5.7, the latest planned release of Microsoft’s JavaScript variant with syntax for types, is now available in a production release. With version 5.7, the language’s type system is now able to report errors when variables have never been initialized.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Archaeologists discover 4,000-year-old canals used to fish by predecessors of ancient Maya
item.description: Using drones and Google Earth imagery, archaeologists have discovered a 4,000-year-old network of earthen canals in what’s now Belize.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Bluesky has a growing problem: fake accounts
item.description: On Wednesday afternoon, billionaire activist investor Bill Ackman appeared to post on Bluesky a plan to destroy the burgeoning social media platform.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple and Google face UK investigation into mobile browser dominance
item.description: A British watchdog says Apple and Google aren't giving consumers a genuine choice of mobile web browsers.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Future of Productivity: Microsoft's Windows 11 Integration on Quest Headsets
item.description: Microsoft announces integration of Windows 11 capabilities with Meta Quest headsets, enhancing productivity and mixed reality experience
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Apple plans for a smarter LLM-based Siri smart assistant
item.description: Once upon a time, we’d say software is eating the planet. It still is, but these days our world is being consumed by generative AI (genAI), which is seemingly being added to everything.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: From vision to value: A strategic approach to generative AI adoption
item.description: Generative AI has the potential to redefine productivity, create novel applications, and reinvent customer experience.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: NetBox Labs launches tools to combat network configuration drift
item.description: NetBox Labs is expanding its network management platform this week with a pair of new products designed to tackle the growing challenges of infrastructure documentation and configuration management.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Opinion: Big Tech betrayed consumer trust by failing to protect our data
item.description: Lawmakers need to set consequences for information-rich companies that outweigh the costs of proper security measures.
item.dc:creator: Devin Jones, The Mercury News

item.title: AI agents are unlike any technology ever
item.description: The agents are coming, and they represent a fundamental shift in the role artificial intelligence plays in businesses, governments, and our lives. The biggest news in agentic AI happened this month when we learned that OpenAI’s agent, Operator, is expected to launch in January.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia rejects Elon Musk's claim that it plans to control access to the internet
item.description: An Australian Cabinet minister has rejected X Corp. owner Elon Musk’s allegation that the government intends to control all Australians' access to the internet through legislation that would ban young children from social media.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A vision for the hospital of the future
item.description: In the grand journey of human progress, healthcare is at a tipping point. Much like the decline of civilizations chronicled by Jared Diamond, traditional hospitals, once viewed as bastions of healing, are on the brink of an existential collapse.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Clozel, Fast Company

item.title: Harnessing AI: A NetApp perspective
item.description: We take pride in our heritage at NetApp. Our heritage is rooted in developing innovative solutions that address the challenges of storing, managing, and protecting data in a complex IT environment.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Nile unwraps NaaS security features for enterprise customers
item.description: Network-as-a-service startup Nile has added new zero-trust capabilities and tapped Microsoft and Palo Alto Networks to bolster its enterprise security services. Nile was founded by former Cisco CEO John Chambers and Pankaj Patel, Cisco’s former chief development officer.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: A Thai court has dismissed a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist who alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: Jerry Harmer And David Rising, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Volcano on Iceland's Reykjanes Peninsula erupts for the 7th time in a year
item.description: A volcano on the Reykjanes Peninsula in southwestern Iceland is spewing lava from a fissure in its seventh eruption since December.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: US gathers allies to talk AI safety as Trump's vow to undo Biden's AI policy overshadows their work
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump has vowed to repeal President Joe Biden's signature artificial intelligence policy when he returns to the White House for a second term. What that actually means for the future of AI technology remains to be seen.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: How the U.S. Space Force can modernize at a smarter pace
item.description: Modernizing space programs while maintaining operations is one of the most challenging things, and rings true for the U.S. Space Force. The post How the U.S. Space Force can modernize at a smarter pace first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: SSA Faces Challenges with Customer Service and Staffing Levels
item.description: The Social Security Administration (SSA) struggles with customer service and staffing levels, despite budget increases.
item.dc:creator: Drew Friedman, Federal News Network

item.title: Azure Container Apps launches Python, JavaScript interpreters
item.description: Python code interpreter sessions are generally available for Microsoft’s Azure Container Apps serverless platform for running applications and microservices. The service is also offering custom dynamic sessions, with JavaScript code interpreter capabilities in public preview.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Network connectivity: An urgent matter of national security
item.description: Building a network architecture and organizational structure that can respond dynamically to new challenges and technologies is critical. The post Network connectivity: An urgent matter of national security first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft Ignite 2024 — get the latest news and insights
item.description: Microsoft Ignite 2024 kicks off in Chicago and runs Nov. 19-22. If you can’t make it to Chicago, no worries. First, the physical event is sold out, according to the Ignite event page. Second, it’s a hybrid event, so you can attend Ignite virtually.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft upgrades Copilot Studio agent builder tools
item.description: Microsoft at this week’s Ignite conference unveiled new Copilot Studio features aimed at both expanding the functionality of AI agents created with the application and improving the accuracy of outputs.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia's communications minister said Wednesday the government won't force social media users to hand over their personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: ChatGPT’s Windows app beats Microsoft Copilot for productivity
item.description: Microsoft’s Copilot AI assistant appears to be transforming into a chatty AI sidekick, and I’ve seen quite a few Copilot users who aren’t happy about it. Thankfully, there’s now another option for anyone interested in using AI purely for productivity — a full-featured ChatGPT app for Windows PCs.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Microsoft unveils imaging APIs for Windows Copilot Runtime
item.description: Microsoft’s Windows Copilot Runtime, which allows developers to integrate AI capabilities into Windows, is being fitted with AI-backed APIs for image processing. It will also gain access to Phi 3.5 Silica, a custom-built generative AI model for Copilot+ PCs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft pitches AI 'agents' that can perform tasks on their own at Ignite 2024
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella told customers at a conference in Chicago on Tuesday that the company is teaching a new set of artificial intelligence tools how to "act on our behalf across our work and life.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Forget driverless cars. One company wants autonomous helicopters to spray crops and fight fires
item.description: HENNIKER, N.H. (AP) — When Hector Xu was learning to fly a helicopter in college, he recalled having a few “nasty experiences” while trying to navigate at night.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: This tool is made for the laziest parent ever
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson discusses how an innovative Easy-Way kit turns standard strollers electric, simplifying navigation for parents.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Unlocking the Power of Observability in Cloud Computing: A Path to Reliability and Agility
item.description: Exploring the importance of observability in cloud computing, its benefits, and the steps businesses can take to achieve success.
item.dc:creator: Info World
